summary: The reproduction bundle shows that esm.sh blocks a literal `127.0.0.1` target but still fetches loopback-only content when the attacker uses a normal-looking hostname that resolves to `127.0.0.1`.
expected_security_impact: The PoC should show a literal loopback target rejected with HTTP 400, while a domain-mapped loopback target succeeds with HTTP 200 and returns transformed JavaScript containing `EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF` from a loopback-only helper service.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned the upstream repository, checked out commit `1ad31b6352bb0a064ece812f6f360e4850e16051`, built the reproduction image, observed control literal-loopback status `400`, observed bypass loopback-domain status `200`, and returned `var o="EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF";export{o as proof};` from the vulnerable fetch path.
verification_method: The verifier adds an `/etc/hosts` mapping from `loopback.evohunt.test` to `127.0.0.1`, starts a loopback-only HTTP helper server that serves `secret.js`, starts the pinned esm.sh server, sends both the blocked literal-loopback request and the bypass hostname request, and requires both the proof string in the transformed response and a matching helper-server access log entry.
evidence_produced_by_run: Bypass request `http://127.0.0.1:18080/http://loopback.evohunt.test:19080/secret.js` returned HTTP 200 with transformed JavaScript containing `EVOHUNT_SSRF_PROOF`.
The loopback-only helper server received `GET /secret.js HTTP/1.1` during the bypass request.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, passes that checkout into `docker build` as the `targetrepo` named build context, and mounts the same checkout into the container at `/target-repo` for runtime provenance checks.
